comment: SQL injection vulnerability. The query parameter is concatenated into the SQL string via f-string. Use a parameterised query with placeholders.
self.lock is referenced but not initialised in this class. This will fail at runtime.
The logger statement could include more context about the transaction.
Consider raising an exception instead of returning None for a missing user — makes the error condition more explicit.
The _cache dictionary has no bounded size or eviction policy. Memory will grow indefinitely. Consider functools.lru_cache with a maxsize.
Logging the password in plaintext is a critical security issue. Remove the password from the log statement immediately.
This is an N+1 query pattern. Each user triggers an additional query. Use a single join query or a batch fetch with user IDs.
This function is doing too much. Consider extracting the filtering logic into a separate helper.
Good fix replacing the unwrap with proper error propagation. Consider also preserving the file path in the error message for easier debugging.
Large resource limit increase without accompanying justification. Verify this is needed and that cluster capacity is sufficient.
Consider using snake_case consistently for local variables.
The 'in _cache' check and subsequent assignment is not atomic. This function looks non-thread-safe but I did not see any locking elsewhere.
